title: Metastelma miserum
intro: Metastelma miserum, also known as the "poorly miserable metaphor", is a species of flowering plant in the milkweed family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Metastelma miserum include alkaloids such as vincamine, ajmalicine, and leucosine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids. In herbal medicine, Metastelma miserum is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or tincture to treat fever, bronchitis, and other respiratory issues, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote digestion.
